title: [release-v4.2.0-rhel] compat,build: handle docker's preconfigured cacheTo,cacheFrom
body:
This is a backport of #16380 which also impacts podman 4.2, while I initially wrongly assumed the problem was only in podman >= 4.3. It seems we in Amadeus have upgraded our build machine during the holiday season and first day of 2023 many of our builds were broken. I have already internally patched podman, repackaged it, and pushed our own Amadeus podman to all our machine, but I wish to make this fix more official and shipped by Red Hat directly.

I have effectively cherry-picked the commit taken from the 4.3 backport done by @flouthoc in #16822.

Note, for the tracking side, I had opened a Red Hat case for the backport to branch 4.3. It was Red Hat customer case 03387104. This lead to the creation of this bugzilla ticket: https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2152516

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

No

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
> warning [Intel macOS instances are no longer supported!](https://cirrus-ci.org/blog/2022/11/08/sunsetting-intel-macos-instances/)

Should be fixed by https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17005.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *flouthoc*, *Romain-Geissler-1A* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.2.0-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [flouthoc] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Probably needs a rebase to get the macintosh fixes

There are still a few flakes here and there, unlikely to be caused by these changes. What's your integration policy in these cases ? You expect the contributor to restart builds until it ends up all green (if yes, is there a way to restart builds without force pushing another git commit with a different sha1 ?) ? Or red pull requests are ok to be merged when there's no link between test failures and the proposed changes ?

The failure I'm seeing is persisting across re-runs, hence does not seem to be a flake:
```
package gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner
	imports gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/commands/helpers
	imports gocloud.dev/blob/azureblob
	imports github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore: build constraints exclude all Go files in /var/tmp/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore@v1.1.1
package gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner
	imports gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/commands/helpers
	imports gocloud.dev/blob/azureblob
	imports github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore/policy: build constraints exclude all Go files in /var/tmp/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azcore@v1.1.1/policy
package gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner
	imports gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/commands/helpers
	imports gocloud.dev/blob/azureblob
	imports github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azidentity: build constraints exclude all Go files in /var/tmp/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/azidentity@v1.0.0
package gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner
	imports gitlab.com/gitlab-org/gitlab-runner/cache/azure
	imports github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/storage/azblob: build constraints exclude all Go files in /var/tmp/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!azure/azure-sdk-for-go/sdk/storage/azblob@v0.4.1
```
It might need the attention of a gitlab-runner SME.

Hi,

For the build issue wrt gitlab-runner on ubuntu 2110, honestly I don't see how this can be related to this pull request, and I have no idea at all how to fix this.

Cheers,
Romain
Ubuntu tests should be ignored and removed from RHEL branch testing.

> Ubuntu tests should be ignored and removed from RHEL branch testing.

@cevich ^

> Ubuntu tests should be ignored and removed from RHEL branch testing.

Most definitely the gitlab test, that should have a 'only on main' condition.  Otherwise, I'm not sure I agree about entirely stripping out Ubuntu (where it's still enabled).  IIRC, that's the only place where we run our suite with `runc`.  At least at one point, that was an important thing.  Is it still?
